paradoxes.title: Verdana Dual-State Paradox
paradoxes.description: The Battle of Verdana is simultaneously recorded as both a decisive victory and a catastrophic defeat across 7 conflicting timeline branches, creating a recursive causality loop that is erasing 2,100 fragments per hour.
paradoxes.technicalNote: Causality fork detected at timestamp 14:22:07. Both states carry equal quantum memory weight. Standard reconciliation protocols insufficient — requires Archivist Ω-level override.
resolutionSteps: Isolate the 7 conflicting branches from the main timeline cluster
Access the original Battle of Verdana fragment (MF-2239) and establish canonical state
Apply paradox seal to subordinate branches
Monitor fragment drain for 24h post-resolution
paradoxes.riskIfUnresolved: Total loss of Sec-1 memory cluster. Cascade into TL-001 full collapse.

paradoxes.title: Mira Chen Duplicate Identity
paradoxes.description: Subject 'Mira Chen' appears with contradictory biographical records across Sector 3 and Sector 9. Quantum identity collapse imminent if not reconciled within 72 hours.
paradoxes.technicalNote: Two memory cores are simultaneously claiming ownership of the Chen identity. Cross-sector identity anchors are required. Fragment MF-2240 holds the earliest divergence point.
resolutionSteps: Retrieve Chen genealogy record (MF-2223) for baseline identity mapping
Cross-reference Sec-3 and Sec-9 memory cores
Establish primary identity anchor in the older record
Reconcile biographical contradictions using quantum averaging
Apply identity seal to prevent re-forking
paradoxes.riskIfUnresolved: Identity collapse propagates to all Sec-3 biographical records. Loss of 340 fragments/hr continues.

paradoxes.title: Void Core Echo Cascade
paradoxes.description: Temporal echo signals from the Core are amplifying a feedback loop in 4 connected thread strands, degrading coherence across adjacent sectors.
paradoxes.technicalNote: Echo origin point traced to fragment MF-2238. The cascade appears to be triggered by an external signal — possibly the future transmission in MF-2229.
resolutionSteps: Silence the echo by restoring MF-2238 architecture fragment
Triangulate external signal source from MF-2229 transmission log
Install echo dampeners on threads TL-005 strands 1-4
Monitor Core resonance for 48h post-seal
paradoxes.riskIfUnresolved: Echo cascade expands to all Core-adjacent timelines. Potential TL-012 destabilisation.

paradoxes.title: Northern Archive Ghost Memories
paradoxes.description: Destroyed memories from the Northern Archive collapse appear to be re-manifesting as incoherent 'ghost' fragments that overwrite valid records in Sector 6.
paradoxes.technicalNote: Ghost fragments detected in 6 threads. No drain rate currently, but overwrite events are occurring every ~2 hours. May accelerate.
resolutionSteps: Map all ghost fragment intrusion points in Sec-6
Use MF-2234 voice logs to identify original archive structure
Quarantine ghost fragments to isolated buffer zone
Attempt reconstruction from ghost data before purge
paradoxes.riskIfUnresolved: Ghost overwrites corrupt valid Sec-6 records. Timeline TL-008 may become unresolvable.

paradoxes.title: Market Ledger Integrity Loop
paradoxes.description: Economic memory records from Cycles 12-15 are referencing each other in a circular dependency that prevents accurate historical reconstruction.
paradoxes.technicalNote: Circular reference loop between MF-2232 and MF-2226. Root cause is disputed ownership records in the original Market Ledger.
resolutionSteps: Break circular reference by establishing external anchor date
Commission independent fragment for Cycle 12-15 boundary
Update cross-references in both fragments
paradoxes.riskIfUnresolved: Sec-2 economic history becomes unverifiable. May enable fragment forgery.

paradoxes.title: Song Memory Frequency Drift
paradoxes.description: The melodic frequency patterns in Harmony Era song fragments are slowly drifting out of alignment with the Memory Loom's resonance frequency.
paradoxes.technicalNote: Frequency drift of 0.003 Hz/day detected. Not immediately dangerous but will eventually cause song fragments to lose their stabilising properties.
resolutionSteps: Calibrate song fragment frequencies against Loom baseline
Update resonance anchors in MF-2227 lyric archive
paradoxes.riskIfUnresolved: Song fragments lose stabilising effect. Minor but non-zero cascade risk in Sec-4.
